title: Free Notion Alternatives for Linux

proprietary.name: Notion
proprietary.description: An all-in-one workspace combining notes, databases, kanban boards, wikis, and project management. Popular for personal knowledge management and team collaboration, Notion blends documents and structured data into a flexible, block-based interface.

alternatives.name: Obsidian
alternatives.description: A powerful knowledge base application that works on top of plain-text Markdown files stored locally on your device. Obsidian excels at building interconnected notes through bidirectional linking and offers a rich plugin ecosystem that extends its functionality far beyond simple note-taking.
features: Local-first storage with plain Markdown files
Bidirectional linking and interactive graph view
Extensive community plugin ecosystem with 1000+ plugins
Canvas for visual brainstorming and spatial note-taking
Customizable themes and CSS snippets
Full-text search with regex support

alternatives.name: Logseq
alternatives.description: A privacy-first, open-source knowledge management and collaboration platform built around an outliner workflow. Logseq stores everything in local Markdown and Org-mode files, supports bidirectional references, and offers a block-level structure ideal for networked thinking and daily journaling.
features: Open-source and fully local-first architecture
Outliner-based block editing with bidirectional links
Built-in graph view for visualizing knowledge connections
Daily journal workflow for capturing thoughts over time
PDF annotation and highlighting support
Whiteboard feature for visual thinking and diagramming

alternatives.name: Joplin
alternatives.description: An open-source note-taking and to-do application with synchronization capabilities across multiple devices. Joplin supports Markdown editing, end-to-end encryption for secure cloud sync, and can import notes from Evernote. It works well as a straightforward, privacy-respecting notes solution.
features: End-to-end encrypted synchronization across devices
Markdown editor with rich-text preview
Web clipper browser extension for saving articles
Notebook and tag-based organization system
Sync via Nextcloud, Dropbox, OneDrive, or Joplin Cloud
Plugin system for extending core functionality

alternatives.name: AppFlowy
alternatives.description: An open-source alternative to Notion that aims to replicate its block-based editing experience while keeping your data under your control. AppFlowy offers documents, databases, kanban boards, and a calendar view, all in a native application that can be self-hosted for complete data sovereignty.
features: Open-source Notion-style block editor
Built-in database views including grid, kanban, and calendar
Self-hostable for full data ownership and control
Native desktop application with fast performance
Real-time collaboration support
Customizable templates for documents and projects

verdict: Obsidian is the top pick for Linux users who want a mature, extensible knowledge base with an unmatched plugin ecosystem and local Markdown storage. Logseq is ideal for those who prefer an outliner workflow and fully open-source tools. Joplin shines for encrypted cross-device syncing, while AppFlowy is the best choice if you want a direct Notion-like experience with databases and kanban boards in an open-source package.

seo.title: Free Notion Alternatives for Linux in 2026 | Open-Source Note-Taking & Wikis
seo.description: Discover the best free and open-source Notion alternatives for Linux. Compare Obsidian, Logseq, Joplin, and AppFlowy for local-first note-taking, knowledge management, and project organization.
